Alina Wang is amazing! She helped me get through a really hard divorce and the confusing legal process of it all. She made all the information very clear, accessible and handled everything with compassion.  She updated me at every step and let me know what was next. Highly recommend.

My attorney, Tian Tian, is young, professional, and incredibly dedicated. She was very responsive and followed up on every detail of my case. During the trial, she impressively argued against the plaintiff, who had hired two lawyers and a financial expert, and successfully exposed their false or misleading statements in court. I was truly impressed by her confidence, skill, and professionalism!

After years of trying to immigrate from Canada by doing everything ourselves we finally reached out for legal help. We met with Brandon Gillin from Genesis Law Firm. Brandon takes the time to keep you aware of your case and even went to our interview with us at no extra charge. He was with us every step of the way. My wife and I highly recommend Brandon for any and all immigration legal assistance.

Brandon Gillin helped me obtain my lawful permanent residence in the United States. Brandon walked my wife and I through the process and he was always there to answer all of our questions. the slogan "when your in a peril call Rick Merrill" holds true. Would highly recommend to everyone shoot I already do!!

reviews.text: Wow - Mike was wonderful and effective - I hope to never need him again but if I do - I will absolutely contract again - Mike - Thank you.

I got a speeding ticket, 37 over in over 40, I contested the ticket and a court date was set. Less than a week before the scheduled court date I decided that I should get an attorney so I called around and I found Mike. He was very kind and even though I was calling with such short notice Mike assured me he would get the date changed and not to worry. If I would’ve paid the ticket it would’ve cost almost 400 dollars, instead I paid half of that and didn’t even have to go to court. Mike was able to get my ticket dismissed and also did it in record time. Mike is very professional, kind, upfront, and honest. He makes it very easy from start to finish, once you agree to hire him he sends an email with a PayPal link so you can pay without going to his office or having to call back. He corresponds with you by text, if that’s what you choose, making it very easy and efficient. This was such an incredible experience, hopefully I never get another ticket, but if I do Traffic Attorneys of Washington will be my first call. Thank you again Mike!

His response was beyond believable, he had my ticket number researched and followed up his e-mail response with a phone call(before i had an opportunity to read the e-mail)
He assured me that he would diligently work on my behalf to have the ticket reduced/thrown out, and was confident of a successful outcome.
Having been promised the sun, moon and stars by previous attorneys, his phone manner exuded confidence and poise, which persuaded me to instantly agree that choosing Traffic Attorneys of Washington would be the wise choice.
Within weeks, a successful negotiation with the prosecutor to an agreeable fine, on a reduced charge, that left my driving record intact.
Moral of the story is not to be caught speeding, but should the unfortunate happen, a wise investment in Mike’s expertise will save you money in the longterm.

reviews.text:
From the very first phone call, I knew I was in the best hands possible. Working with Robert Silber and his outstanding team of professionals wasn’t just about legal representation—it was an experience that made me feel like family, not just a client.

Robert’s knowledge, dedication, and compassion go far beyond the expectations I had going into this process. He took the time to listen to every detail, answer every question, and guide me with complete honesty and care. His team—each and every one of them—was attentive, professional, and genuinely invested in my well-being. I never once felt like a case number; I felt like a person they truly cared about.

Throughout the 4+ years of my case, they were tenacious, strategic, and tireless in their work, making sure my voice was heard and my rights were protected. Communication was clear and consistent, and I always knew where I stood. They walked me through every step with empathy and confidence, which brought me so much peace during a very difficult time.

If you’re looking for an attorney who will stand by you, fight for you, and treat you like one of their own, look no further than Robert Silber and his team. Their integrity, heart, and skill are second to none. I am endlessly grateful for what they’ve done for me. They are more than legal professionals—they are truly exceptional human beings.

I will never regret my decision to hire Robert Silber for representation.

I worked with the Staton Silber team for almost 2 years throughout the time they were helpful and always would be there to tell me updates/email/call back in a prompt manner and help with any concerns/questions I had.  It made me have a peace of mind  knowing they were there to help me through a difficult season of navigating through L&I. I am so very thankful for all they did to get to know me and my story and help advocate for what I needed throughout this whole process. Being on the other side now I am so glad I went to Staton Silber to help and I am very grateful for all there time they poured into me and my case to help it end well. Thank you Staton Silber and for the ones I worked more close with I appreciate all you did!

Robert is the man. I came to Robert towards the end of my L&I case. I thought I should talk to someone with some experience with L&I cases before I made a decision. Boy, was I right. L&I make it appear that the conclusions are black and white. There is plenty of grey area. At our first meeting, Robert explained how my case would play out. He was absolutely spot on.  Thank you Robert!

I was trying to reopen an L&I claim, I spoke to Robert for the interview about June 20th he was a little sarcastic as he was listening to me explaining my claim, I never hear back from him after that, I call his office a bunch of times just to hear that he was not there or he was busy, on August 13th after almost two months his assistant call me just to tell me that he said he was not able to take my claim due to complexities and he already have to many on his hands, I miss the deadline time to reopen my claim just because he was not professional enough to let me know on time or it could it be just bad luck.

Wow, where to start with this workers comp law firm. For anyone who's considering this firm, I was referred to Staton Silber by one of Washington state top workers comp law firm. The referring firm couldn't take my case because of their current case load at that time. (What a blessing for me) And the referring firm spoke highly of Staton Silber.

With that being said, choosing a workers comp lawyer or law firm is a very serious and important decision. Everything your fighting for is on the line. I have been a client of Staton Silber for over 5years. I can say to you when hiring this firm, your getting the (Young Mike Tyson) of workers comp law firms. They will NOT stop fighting for you under any circumstances. They can't and won't be bullied by the size of an employer or the filing process. Staton Silber will ensure that LNI and the employer abide by the law every step of the way. I've had the privilege of working closely with both attorneys, as their client. Natasha and Robert are two of the most professional attorneys that you can ever choose. Natasha and Robert has made sure to hire a staff that compliments the firm professionalism. Kristine and Cora are a great asset to the Staton Silber Law Firm. My interactions with everyone has always been nothing short of excellent. Having Natasha fighting on my side has made this case a lot easier to deal with and sleep at night. Going through the workers comp process is extremely stressful and you should have a great lawyer to help you navigate the complications of the LNI process. Staton Silber will be there to guide you and keep you informed each and every step of the way. Any questions I've had that couldn't be answered by the firm very capable assistance Kristine, has been promptly answered by Natasha or Robert. On a scale of 1-10, I give Staton Silber a 20. Which is obviously above the scale, that's what you can expect from Staton Silber. What a blessing this firm has been for me. So to sum this review up. If you are looking for the best, look no further. Staton Silber is the very best. So for the firm and everyone there,👍👍👍. Thank you very much.
Very helpful and knowledgeable!

My wife and I were rear-ended by a dump truck 3 years ago. Our car as well as 3 others were totaled. She had major internal injuries and I, her husband, had minor injuries. Pat Trudell's firm along with his staff dug into the details and got us a fair award from the truck company. They were there to support us all along the way with updates and to make sure we were taken care of medically and in every other way. We were very happy with their care and concern for us.
Patrick A. Trudeau, P.S. and his staff are fantastic. He's been my Lawyer for years.  I will go to him if I ever have a need again.
They have always been amazing. I literally tell everyone I know who needs a lawyer to go to this office.
Great people and would be beneficial if you need their help.

Stephen represented me in an auto accident, so when our Autistic daughter was VICOUSLY assaulted at Roxbury Elementary School by a student named Alex we asjed Mr. Lingenbrink to represent us against the school district which he accepted.  He never told us he'd done work for them.  The sub teacher who witnessed the assault had a statement as well as her bus driver who stated she came on the bus unscathed in the a.m. but came on the bus after school bloody & bruised.  Then after hiring Stephen he dragged & dragged it out saying he was workings on the case.   Then all of the sudden the Seattle School District retracted that it happened, the sub teacher got reassigned & his statement mysteriously disappeared as well as the bus drivers!  Then CPS turns it all around on us trying to say we had assaulted her before she came on the bus!!?? So CPS finally declares it neutral that they didn't know who had assaulted her even with multiple witness statements stating it was the school's negligence.   Then Stephen decides not to take the case!!  So I do some searching & low & behold found Mr. Lingenbrink was very involved with the district.  I believe wholeheartedly to this day that he conspired with the district, dragged us on and on so we couldn't sue. I wouldn't doubt for a minute he got paid off somehow.
15 years later our daughter is still permanently affected & has actual scars by the assault & zero trust in Seattle schools & we all view CPS as necessary but heavily corrupt.  The Seattle School district literally covered up their complicity in the assault to not get sued, and protecting themselves permanently scarred a child for life.  Thanks Steve, I hope karma catches up to you one day, what you did was pure evil.

Steve Lingenbrink and his staff exemplify what legal advocacy should be about. They are caring, attentive and conscientious. Steve is real, down to earth and a tireless worker. You can do no better than this crew, trust me.

Some close friends received some losses and small from being run off the freeway. They are older and didn't know what to do as there other parties insurance was making an offer to settle. I have known Steve Lingenbrink for years and recommended they just go talk to him. After my friends came back from Steve they were very happy and satisfied with his advise and treatment. They received a much better and fair settlement that speed up there recovery. I would recommend Steve again if anyone I know is hurt or in an accident. Nicest Attorney in Kirkland.

Barely OK service and quite expensive. The admin is not very punctual either.
If you dare to leave a review which is not to the lawyer's satisfaction, he will make sure to send a personal email to express his dissatisfaction.

Stay away from this firm! This firm clearly doesn't care about the customer needs. Very poor experience with scheduling a consultation call with them and the follow through.

Their overall methodology is to throw technology at you (questionnaires, fill out forms etc.) to help streamline their process. They don't care about your needs and want to put you through their "pipeline".

Pay heed to some of the other poor google reviews on this site about this firm. They are unlike any other professional law firm I have interacted with.

If i could give negative stars i would.. this is one of the worst possible law firms to try to have business with...
I had a v simple task of getting a basic will written, i reach out to the law firm. .they give me a questionairre to fill and give me a date after a few months.. I fill up the questionaire, pay the money and wait for my date
A week before my appointment, they call back asking that if i can reschedule owing to some issues at their end, i agree. .but the next date is months later.. i agree since i am in no hurry. I ask them if they can confirm that they have all the docs and details at their end in the meantime and get a confirmation..

With a week left for appointment,  i had a medical emergency and had to go to ER for surgery and was not checking my mails..
They sent a mail that they need more details and in less than 48 hours sent another mail saying, they have cancelled my appointment.. as the doc was not submitted... something they could have checked and replied to months ago...not even a call..while happily sitting on the money i paid months ago

Pretty neat scam i must say!!

Our experience with Vijay and his office was disappointing and frustrating. In my opinion, it seems like Vijay's practice is designed for clients who fit within a streamlined, cookie-cutter process, where volume is prioritized over individualized service—at least this was the impression we received from his front office staff. Despite being told by our legal plan that the wait time would be up to 3 weeks, we were informed after contacting the office that we would need to wait 9 weeks for an appointment. When we tried to gather basic information about the services provided, the paralegal, Grace, was curt and unwilling to engage in a conversation about whether Vijay could handle our specific needs. This lack of transparency made it difficult for us to make an informed decision about whether working with Vijay would be the right fit. Ultimately, after a long wait, we were told that Vijay would not be able to assist with our estate planning. The entire process felt inefficient and left us feeling as though our concerns were not taken seriously.

In my opinion, Vijay would benefit from improving the training of his front office staff to handle situations like ours more professionally. While I understand that Vijay may have had positive experiences with other clients, this was certainly not the case for us. Having worked with many attorneys over the years, I can confidently say that this was not a typical experience.

We really enjoyed working with Vijay and Grace. They communicated clearly, answered all of our questions, and were very kind. The information we provided through their online portal seemed to make the estate planning process easier, and I really appreciated how thorough the paperwork was (and Vijay's explanation of it). Their office has a ton of parking, which was a nice perk when we had to go for the signing!

Working with Grace and Vijay on our family's estate plan was super streamlined and the price was very reasonable!  The online templates provided to start the process were easy to follow, our subsequent calls were very informative and meeting in person to sign the final documents was super organized.  Highly recommend working with this firm!

I requested Vijay to provide legal services. He was thorough, patient and informative. I asked some same questions repeatedly, but he took the time to clarify and speak in non-legal language and validated I understood the options. The office assistants were quick to respond and also very helpful in ensuring timely closure of my particular needs. Will be referring friends and family for future needs.
Ramon, Vijay and Eva thoroughly and professionally answered my tons of questions. I thought the service here was great.
Vijay was very responsive to all of our needs, he does a great job putting things in layman's terms and ensuring all parties are on the same page. This was a smooth and easy process, thank you!

Mr Gosalia handled our trust planning and was very clear in explaining the causes. Due to covid, we had to have all our discussions over email and phone. He was available to explain the same thing more than once or twice, patiently. Scheduling appointments was also easy. They did respond quickly to my email questions seeking clarifications. I would definitely recommend Mr Vijay Gosalia for your trust planning.

I contacted this firm to get help with a tax issue. First time the receptionist was nice enough. It got very difficult to get any response from them after that. Second time I called,  the receptionist was incredibly rude. Needless  to say, I would not hire them.

After relocating to the PNW and buying a home, my financial advisor suggested that my wife and I create a Joint Revocable Living Trust. After doing some research I contacted Ortiz & Gosalia and initially spoke with Kasey who manages the office. She sent me some information to complete, and not long after submission she called me to schedule an in person appointment with Vijay Gosalia, the attorney that we'd be working with to create the JRLT. Vijay took his time to explain the differences between a standard will and trust, and discussed our needs so we could make an informed decision. From the very beginning, we felt that we were in great hands. The team was very professional, highly responsive, and what I would consider subject matter experts. Vijay was very thoughtful in his approach and made discussing what most would consider a very sensitive subject...easy! I highly recommend Ortiz & Gosalia if you are in the market for estate planning or any other service they offer.

I retained this attorney to complete a limited scope matter: finalizing paperwork related to a CR2A that had already been completed. I paid over $10,000 for this work.
From the beginning, communication was inconsistent. Scheduled calls were frequently postponed or canceled, and billing was not provided in a timely or transparent manner. Charges continued to appear long after substantive work on my case had ended, and invoices trickled in over the course of more than a year.
I repeatedly requested a final bill, but billing continued even when no additional work was being performed. When I ultimately disputed additional charges totaling several thousand dollars and declined to pay them, her office pushed back but then communication from the attorney ceased entirely.
I was also told the attorney was “preparing for trial,” despite the fact that the matter was governed by an existing CR2A and was never procedurally headed toward trial.
Additionally, I was informed that opposing counsel was a former college roommate of this attorney. While I was told this did not constitute a conflict of interest, it is something that I believe was a conflict of interest and the case dragged on unnecessarily. Her paralegal even went to work for the other attorney while she was still on my case.
Based on my experience, I cannot recommend this attorney. I paid a substantial amount of money and did not receive clear value, consistent communication, or reliable billing practices.

I worked with Jenny through the Washington State Moderate Means Program on a vehicle engine damage case that lasted a little over two years. Overall, Jenny is a competent, knowledgeable attorney who understands civil litigation, insurance dynamics, and settlement processes well.

She successfully brought my case to resolution, navigated delays from opposing counsel, and ensured that the settlement was ultimately completed and disbursed. From a technical and legal standpoint, the case was handled properly, and I do believe she put in substantial work over the life of the matter.

That said, I think it’s important to be candid about the Moderate Means experience specifically. From the beginning, I was transparent about my limited financial situation and that I was relying on settlement proceeds to cover attorney fees. While the reduced hourly rate was honored, there were repeated requests to make payments or enter open-ended payment plans, which was difficult given the financial realities that led me to the Moderate Means program in the first place.

At times, this created tension, particularly later in the case when settlement discussions overlapped with billing pressure. While I don’t believe there was any bad faith, it sometimes felt as though the financial side of the representation overshadowed client reassurance and alignment, which can be challenging for clients of limited means.

In short, Jenny is still 5 Stars in my book, a capable attorney who will do the work and get a case across the finish line. For Moderate Means clients, clearer upfront expectations around billing pressure, timing, and flexibility would greatly improve the experience and better reflect the spirit of the program.

I appreciate the work that was done and the eventual outcome, while also hoping this feedback helps strengthen access-to-justice representation for future clients.

Hired these guys several years ago in 2014 for my first and only DUI that happened in Bellevue. Police had no dash cam footage, lacked probable cause to pull me over in the first place but somehow they completely lost my case. Not sure if it's cause of inexperience on who was assigned to me (Denis Globa) but I wasn't happy. I ended up paying more in court fines and punishment than my friend who had a lousy public defender. (DUI, $4,000 in fines, license suspended, and 15 days electronic home monitoring) I don't recommend them at all.

Edit: Of course you dispute the validity, I would too if it wasn't glowing. However your firm was not effective in even making a reasonable deal for a first time offense. Denis discouraged me from jury trial under your direction, and I was made by the court to pay more in fines than a few of my other friends. Discussing my case with you now won't change anything, but other potential clients should be warned that they may have better results from a public defender.
reviews.ownerResponse:
My firm always strives to provide the best representation possible for each and every client and that is evident in all the reviews we have received dating back to 2005. Anytime someone has concerns about their case, it is of utmost importance to me as a business owner.

I would have very much appreciated the opportunity to address your concerns in 2014 as opposed to having to respond to a review 4 years after the case had concluded.

Feel free to call me and I'll be happy to review the case with you again and give you my opinions about how it was handled and if anything different should have been done by Mr. Globa.

As the reviewer clarified, this case took place and was concluded in 2014 by an associate who is no longer with my firm, however, I was surprised to see this review written four years later in 2018. Therefore, I only recently became aware of the reviewer's concerns. When a review is written years later rather than an individual discussing any issues they may have at the time with me directly, it certainly proves difficult to rectify those concerns and to ensure the case was handled within their expectations.

We never guarantee any particular outcome, but we do guarantee we will argue viable issues to the court and put the individual in the best position possible given the evidence posed against them. Bellevue Police Department did not have dash or body cameras in 2014 and certainly it is unfair to individuals who dispute whether there was reasonable suspicion to stop them or probable cause to arrest. As you can imagine, in preliminary arguments (prior to any jury trial) a judge makes those determinations based upon the officer's testimony (again not a very fair proposition when it is a citizen's word against that of an officer). It appears that issue was litigated in this case and it was the judge who made the final determination as to the basis for the stop and probable cause to arrest after hearing testimony. I'm sorry the judge didn't rule in your favor on this case.

Regarding the fines and fees you indicated were assessed, the court imposes the particular fees and costs without a lawyer's input or argument, absent establishing the individual is indigent. I did not attend the hearings as you elected to hire my associate but am familiar with that court and the imposition of fines and court costs. I did review the judgment and sentence wherein the court totaled the fines and fees and what is stated in the review that was imposed is not consistent with what was on that judgment and sentence; if you were overcharged by the court, I'm happy to assist you in retrieving any overcharge.

It is my practice that when any client has questions about the possible outcomes, the criminal implications, and/or the impact on their driving privilege, my firm takes the time to discuss those in great detail, we then offer our advice on how to proceed and in the end assist the client in making the decision that is in their best interest given all the surrounding circumstances and potential evidence which would be presented at a jury trial.

My firm has excelled at DUI defense and been consistent in our firm motto with over 1000 clients throughout 14 years. We do our utmost to put people in the best position possible, always have and always will.

reviews.text: We'd like to share our experience with Kesselman Law Firm. We received expert advice from Roman during our first meeting, after which we signed a contract for his defense. We were well prepared for the trial, which allowed us to calmly and confidently attend the hearing. As a result, our family was granted asylum on October 9, 2025, thanks to the professional work of Roman and his team.

Our experience with KESSELMAN LAW FIRM was mixed. We initially worked with attorney Zara, and unfortunately, it was quite disappointing. She delayed submitting our documents for nearly three months, during which we had to follow up every week just to get updates. Each time, she told us the paperwork was already submitted, but we later discovered it was not. Whenever we asked her questions—especially about the green card process and what we could or could not do based on my wife’s green card status—she either avoided answering or admitted uncertainty, even though the official immigration website made the process quite clear. It left us questioning whether her knowledge was limited or if she simply wasn’t attentive to our case. Honestly, we found ourselves relying more on ChatGPT throughout the process, which provided more accurate and timely guidance than Zara did.

A couple of months later, we had an urgent family situation and needed to apply for advance parole for my wife. At that point, our case was transferred to Elina, and the experience changed completely. She was responsive, resourceful, and very helpful. Elina explained the process clearly, followed up without us having to chase her, and gave us confidence that our case was being handled properly.

Overall, if you decide to work with KESSELMAN LAW FIRM, I strongly recommend asking to work with Elina. Based on our experience, she is professional, knowledgeable, and truly cares about her clients.
